  dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1 | cat
arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

To collect crash dumps on .NET, the standard approach is to use environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory). Options: 1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump


These variables instruct the .NET runtime to automatically generate a crash dump when the process crashes. I should verify that `dotnet` is available and check its version, then run the tests with these environment variables configured.

  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/tmp/sv-58bb3d2096614caf9194734695fa179a/crashdump_%p.dmp \
  dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1 | cat

data.content:
Tests passed. Here's the command to run your tests with crash dump collection:

```bash
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/tmp/sv-58bb3d2096614caf9194734695fa179a/crashdump_%p.dmp \
dotnet test
```

**What the environment variables do:**

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` | Enables automatic dump on crash |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` | Full memory dump (use `1` for mini, `2` for heap, `3` for triage) |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=..._%p.dmp` | Dump path; `%p` is replaced with the crashed process PID |

If a test crashes the host, a `.dmp` file will be written to the project directory. All tests passed successfully this time — no dump was generated.
